method_atom: A multiscale coupling scheme that lets the level of description (number of degrees of freedom) vary smoothly across space using an auxiliary interpolating 'switching' function to blend a fine-grained and a coarse-grained representation, allowing free migration of units between regions. Two coupling strategies are contrasted — interpolating the interaction forces versus interpolating the interaction potential — together with a consistency argument showing that the potential-interpolation strategy cannot support a conservative (energy-conserving) formulation, whereas the force-interpolation one can.
object_atom: Classical molecular dynamics simulations of interacting particles (atoms/molecules), in which high-resolution (atomistic) and low-resolution (coarse-grained) spatial regions coexist and particles exchange freely between them.
brief_reason: The transferable machinery is the switching-function interpolation between two coupled descriptions of differing granularity plus the energy-conservation consistency analysis; the domain-specific object is the classical MD particle system with adaptive atomistic/coarse-grained regions.
